rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the actual ground-truth concern (yenuo26's question about whether reduce_scatter needs deeper test verification beyond hasattr checks), so recall is low for both. X produces five nitpick-level comments (test coverage, docstrings, a debatable 'dead code' claim) but its own verdict (REQUEST CHANGES) contradicts its internal next_action field ('ready to merge as-is; no changes required'), and the review_text is oddly duplicated three times verbatim, undermining coherence. Y verifies the actual vLLM API contracts (DPMetadata fields, init_model_parallel_group signature, destroy logic) with grounded, correct claims, honestly flags its sandbox limitations, and lands on APPROVE with one well-placed optional comment — matching the real outcome (Gaohan123's LGTM approval) far better than X's overwrought REQUEST CHANGES.
